data.title: CHAPTER 3 — CONSTRAINTS AND BOUNDARIES
blocks: A researcher asks Claude to summarize the evidence on a contested medical topic. Claude returns four paragraphs: confide
Chapter 3 — Constraints and Boundaries — The prompt never said to use only the provided sources. The prompt never said what to do with uncertainty. Claude perfor

S02.meta: // SLIDE 02 — WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO

data.title: WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO.
blocks: · Define the scope of a task so Claude stays inside the job and does not invent adjacent material
· Set evidence rules so Claude draws only on what you provide
· Require uncertainty disclosure when evidence is missing or incomplete
· Protect private or sensitive data from appearing in outputs
· Specify a tone or register that must not drift
data.section: WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO

data.title: WHAT A CONSTRAINT IS.
blocks: Scope constraints
Evidence constraints
Uncertainty constraints
Privacy and ethics constraints
Tone and register constraints

S04.meta: // SLIDE 04 — BEFORE AND AFTER: ADDING CONST

data.title: BEFORE AND AFTER: ADDING CONSTRAINTS TO A PROMPT
blocks: · Whether it should confine itself to sources the user provides
· What to do when evidence is mixed or contested
· Whether the user wants confidence levels or just conclusions
· How long the output should be
· Whether "productivity" means self-reported satisfaction, output measurement, or something else

data.title: THE "DO NOT INVENT" INSTRUCTION.
blocks: · "If a specific fact, figure, or name is not in the materials I have provided, do not include it."
· "If you would need to guess, say 'I do not have a source for this' and stop."
· "Do not add context from your training that was not given in this prompt."
· "If you are uncertain about a claim, mark it with and I will verify it."

S06.meta: // SLIDE 06 — CONSTRAINTS ON REFUSAL: WHAT T

data.title: CONSTRAINTS ON REFUSAL: WHAT TO DO AT THE BOUNDARY
blocks: "Refuse or ask" rule:
"Flag and continue" rule:
"Narrow the scope" rule:

data.title: CONSTRAINTS THAT CONFLICT.
blocks: Resolution by priority:
Resolution by exception:
Resolution by query:

data.title: THE HUMAN GATE.
blocks: · Is every claim in these materials traceable to a provided source?
· Is every uncertain claim flagged?
· Is the output within the specified word count?
· Does the tone hold throughout?
· Has any private information appeared in the output?

data.title: COMMON MISTAKES
blocks: Stating the task without any constraints.
Using constraints that are too vague to enforce.
Setting too many constraints without resolution order.
Assuming Claude remembers constraints from earlier in a conversation.
Treating a constraint as a substitution for judgment.
